summary: A major milestone — AI Settings completely overhauled with 9 BYOK providers, a cleaner interface, and smarter compatibility warnings so you always know your setup is working.

items.title: WiseResume AI test results are now clearly branded
items.description: When you tap 'Test AI Connection' in Settings, the result now shows a clean 'WiseResume AI' confirmation — no raw model names or internal provider IDs. You simply see that your AI is working.

items.title: Tool-calling compatibility warning
items.description: If you configure a BYOK model that doesn't support advanced AI features — such as Ollama Cloud or certain base models — a warning chip now appears upfront before you run into a silent failure. WiseResume tells you early so you can pick a compatible model.

items.title: AI Settings redesigned with two clear paths
items.description: The AI Settings panel is now split into two distinct sections: WiseResume AI (managed, no setup needed) and BYOK (bring your own key). Less clutter, easier to switch, and much clearer what each option means.

items.title: All 9 BYOK providers share one consistent setup flow
items.description: No matter which provider you choose — OpenAI, Anthropic, Groq, Mistral, or any of the others — the setup experience is the same: enter your key, pick a model, validate. Done.

items.title: Reliability improvements throughout
items.description: Multiple edge cases in BYOK key validation, model fetching, and AI routing have been resolved. The AI connection test is more stable and the fallback behaviour is more predictable across all providers.

summary: BYOK now supports 9 providers — including OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, Groq, Mistral, and more — with a new unified picker and live model lists pulled directly from each provider.

items.title: 7 new BYOK providers added
items.description: You can now connect your own API key from OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, Groq, Mistral, xAI Grok, Cohere, and OpenRouter — in addition to the existing Gemini and Ollama support. That's 9 providers total.

items.title: New unified provider picker
items.description: All BYOK providers now live inside one clean interface. Browse, select, and switch between providers in a single place instead of hunting through separate sections.

items.title: Live model lists fetched from each provider
items.description: Instead of a fixed dropdown, model options are now fetched live from each provider's API. You always see the models actually available on your key — not a hardcoded list that may be out of date.

items.title: Anthropic Claude fully supported for all AI features
items.description: Claude Sonnet, Haiku, and Opus now work for every AI action in WiseResume — including resume parsing, tailoring, ATS scoring, and structured improvements. All features that previously required workarounds now run cleanly.

summary: Pro and Pro+ features are now clearly marked across the app, with smart upgrade prompts and resume creation limits enforced per plan.

items.title: Premium features visually flagged throughout the app
items.description: Pro-only and Pro+-only features now have clear visual indicators wherever they appear — so you always know at a glance what's included in your plan and what requires an upgrade.

items.title: Smart upgrade prompts instead of silent failures
items.description: Attempting to use a feature beyond your plan now shows a friendly, informative prompt explaining what you'd get with an upgrade — rather than a confusing error or nothing happening at all.

items.title: Resume creation limits enforced by plan
items.description: Free plan users can see their remaining resume slots at a glance and are guided toward upgrading when they've reached the limit, with a clear explanation of what each plan includes.

items.title: Faster support for plan changes
items.description: Plan changes and trial grants can now be applied directly by the support team, which means quicker resolution if you ever need a manual plan adjustment.

summary: WiseResume now has three clear subscription tiers — Free, Pro, and Pro+ — with a new Subscription page where you can see your plan, usage, and upgrade in one place.

items.title: New pricing tiers: Free, Pro, and Pro+
items.description: WiseResume now offers three plans with a clear breakdown of what each includes. Whether you're just getting started or running a full job search, there's a tier that fits.

items.title: New Subscription page
items.description: A dedicated Subscription page shows your current plan, AI credit usage, and renewal status all in one place. Upgrade or start a trial directly from the same page — no digging through settings.

items.title: Trial access for eligible users
items.description: Eligible users can now activate a limited-time trial of Pro or Pro+ directly from the Subscription page to try the full feature set before committing.

items.title: AI credit tracking
items.description: AI credits used per action are now tracked and visible in your account, so you always know where you stand and can plan your usage accordingly.

summary: Import your LinkedIn profile when creating a resume, and enjoy a faster, more reliable WiseResume AI powered by a new dual-engine system.

items.title: LinkedIn import in Create Resume
items.description: When creating a new resume, you can now pull your LinkedIn profile directly into WiseResume as a starting point — saving you from filling everything in by hand. Just paste your profile and let WiseResume do the rest.

items.title: WiseResume AI is faster and more reliable
items.description: The AI engine now runs on a dual-AI system with automatic fallback. This means faster responses day-to-day and fewer interruptions if one engine is temporarily busy — your work keeps moving without delays.

items.title: AI connection test edge case fixed
items.description: The 'Test AI Connection' button in Settings was occasionally returning a confusing error for WiseResume AI users. This is now fixed — the test connects cleanly every time.

summary: A quick stability and polish update — the Interview Prep page no longer crashes, and the empty dashboard now shows real previews of your future resume.

items.title: Interview Prep page no longer crashes on open
items.description: Navigating to Interview Prep was crashing the entire page every time due to a router incompatibility in the back-navigation guard. It's now fixed — the page opens reliably and the back button still works as expected.

items.title: Microphone test error no longer silently fails
items.description: In the Interview Setup screen, if the microphone permission check encountered an error it would fail silently with no feedback. It now correctly shows a toast notification explaining what went wrong.

items.title: Empty dashboard shows real template previews
items.description: When you haven't created a resume yet, the dashboard's 'No Resumes Yet' screen previously showed placeholder bars that looked nothing like actual templates. It now shows properly scaled-down previews of the Modern, Classic, and Minimal templates — exactly as they appear in the app.

summary: A behind-the-scenes reliability update — uploading your resume, saving your profile, and testing your AI connection all work more smoothly now.

items.title: Resume upload no longer gets stuck
items.description: If the AI was slow or temporarily unreachable while you uploaded a PDF, the upload would sometimes fail completely. It now always finishes — using a built-in fallback to pull your information even without AI, so you never lose your upload.

items.title: Profile changes now save properly
items.description: Your name, job title, location, and other profile details weren't always saving correctly. This is now fixed — hit Save and your changes stick right away.

items.title: Small label fix in Settings
items.description: The sign-in method in your Account settings was showing 'Kinde' (our auth system's name) instead of 'Email'. It now correctly says 'Email' — much clearer.

items.title: AI connection test works again
items.description: The 'Test AI Connection' button in Settings was returning a 'Model not found' error for WiseResume AI users. That's fixed — the test now connects cleanly and confirms your AI is working.

summary: The home screen now knows who you are — returning users get a personalised quick-launch hub, while new visitors see a clean, focused app-store-style landing page.

items.title: Personalised home for signed-in users
items.description: When you open WiseResume while logged in, you now land on a personal hub with a 'Hey [Name] 👋' greeting and a quick-action grid — My Resumes, Tailor to Job, My Portfolio, and Interview Prep — instead of a marketing page. No more scrolling past features you already know.

items.title: Cleaner guest landing page
items.description: Visitors who haven't signed up yet now see a streamlined, app-store-style landing with a prominent Sign Up button and a subtle Log In link — making the first step crystal clear.

items.title: Live AI editor demo in 'See It in Action'
items.description: The animated AI Resume Editor demo now loops continuously in the 'See It in Action' section — watch it type, enhance, and score a bullet in real time — right alongside the Portfolio Builder demo.

summary: A quick polish pass -- the splash screen now features a twinkling star-field and you can replay it any time from Settings.

items.title: Star-field splash background
items.description: Thirty softly twinkling stars drift inward toward the logo during the splash animation, with a faint nebula glow behind them for a cinematic space feel.

items.title: Replay Splash Screen in Settings
items.description: A new button in Settings > About & Help lets you re-watch the animated intro whenever you want.

items.title: Reduced-motion compliance
items.description: All new splash animations are fully disabled when the OS-level Reduce Motion preference is active -- stars render as static dots instead.

summary: The portfolio just became a jaw-dropping experience — cinematic animations, a live career timeline, a Contact Me button, and a full UX audit pass across the editor and dashboard.

items.title: Portfolio hero cinematic entrance
items.description: The name now reveals letter by letter, the profile photo scales in with a breathing glow ring, the job title badge bounces in, and the CTA buttons stagger up in sequence on every page load.

items.title: Typewriter tagline fixed & upgraded
items.description: The hero tagline no longer cuts off mid-word. Phrases cycle cleanly with a properly blinking cursor, configurable from portfolio settings, falling back to the user's top skills automatically.

items.title: Ambient hero background
items.description: A slow aurora gradient breathes behind the hero section, cycling through deep dark tones for a cinematic atmosphere without distracting from content.

items.title: Scroll-triggered animations
items.description: Every section title draws an underline as it enters view, experience cards slide in staggered, skill tags bubble-pop in one by one, and education cards fade up from below — all powered by IntersectionObserver, never layout-blocking.

items.title: Card hover lift & skill tag interactions
items.description: Experience and education cards lift off the page on hover with a deepening shadow. Skill tags glow to accent color on desktop hover and bounce with a physical press feel on mobile tap.

items.title: Line-by-line bio reveal
items.description: The About section bio uncovers itself line by line like a curtain lifting, and the "Show more" accordion in experience cards unfolds smoothly with a chevron rotation.

items.title: Button shimmer sweep
items.description: The "Share Card" and "Download CV" buttons in the hero emit a periodic diagonal light shimmer when idle — alive without being distracting.

items.title: Sticky header slide-in
items.description: When scrolling past the hero, the sticky name/avatar bar now slides down cinematically with a backdrop blur instead of snapping into view abruptly.

items.title: Career Card 3D flip & holographic shimmer
items.description: Switching between Cosmic, Aurora, and Clean card styles triggers a 3D flip transition. The card preview gains a continuous iridescent shimmer and tilts toward the cursor or touch point.

items.title: Career timeline with scroll-draw
items.description: A vertical accent-colored timeline line draws downward through the experience section as you scroll, with dots that pop in alongside each card, giving visitors an instant feel for career progression.

items.title: "Open to Work" availability badge
items.description: If the user has set their availability in portfolio settings, a pulsing green dot badge now appears in the hero automatically, visible to every recruiter who visits the portfolio.

items.title: Animated stats strip
items.description: A new "Years Experience / Roles Held / Skills" strip between the hero and About section counts up from zero when it enters the viewport, with staggered timing per stat.

items.title: Section quick-nav pills
items.description: A sticky horizontally scrollable pill row on mobile lets visitors jump directly to any section with one tap. The active pill updates automatically as you scroll.

items.title: "Contact Me" sticky CTA
items.description: A floating pill button appears after the hero scrolls out of view, resolving the best contact method automatically (email → WhatsApp → LinkedIn). Pulses subtly every 5 seconds to catch attention without being intrusive.

items.title: Editor sync conflict fixed
items.description: The "Sync Conflict Detected" modal no longer appears when clicking "+ Add" in any section. Conflict detection now only triggers on genuine server/local timestamp mismatches, and the dismiss button works on the first tap.

items.title: Studio page crash resolved
items.description: The AI Studio page no longer throws "supabaseUrl is required" on load. Supabase client initialization is now properly guarded before the page mounts.

items.title: Activity URL redirect
items.description: Navigating directly to /activity now correctly redirects to /applications instead of showing a 404 error page.

items.title: Bottom tab navigation
items.description: The top navigation links have been replaced with a mobile-native fixed bottom tab bar on screens below 1024px, matching standard mobile app conventions. Desktop retains the existing top nav layout.

items.title: Duplicate FAB removed
items.description: The floating "+" button on the Home dashboard has been removed. The "New Resume" button in the action row is the single clear entry point for resume creation.

items.title: Resume name in editor header
items.description: The active resume name is now visible and tappable in the Editor header, making it immediately clear which resume is being edited and allowing quick navigation back to the dashboard.

items.title: Save vs. Unpublish button differentiation
items.description: On the Portfolio page, "Save Portfolio" remains the bold primary action while "Unpublish Portfolio" is now styled as a bordered destructive button with a warning icon — the two actions are no longer visually identical.

items.title: All animations respect reduced motion
items.description: Every animation introduced in this release is fully disabled when the user has "Reduce Motion" enabled at the OS level, ensuring full accessibility compliance.

summary: Smoother animations, a refreshed Settings footer, and better tap targets -- the app feels faster and looks sharper.

items.title: Smoother border animations
items.description: The glowing border effect now uses fewer draw calls on smaller screens, so scrolling and transitions feel snappier -- especially on older phones.

items.title: No more white flash on launch
items.description: Opening the app in dark mode no longer briefly flashes a white screen before your content appears.

items.title: Redesigned About card in Settings
items.description: The WiseResume version card at the bottom of Settings now has a glowing app icon, a version badge, and a proper Changelog button.

items.title: Bigger tap targets across the app
items.description: Buttons and icons that were slightly too small for comfortable thumb use now meet the 44x44px accessibility standard.

summary: A focused mobile polish pass -- the portfolio page is cleaner, the install banner stays out of the way, and empty states guide you forward.

items.title: Portfolio Overview hero card
items.description: Status, public link, stats, and publish toggle are now combined into one compact card at the top of the Portfolio page.

items.title: Empty sections hide automatically
items.description: Portfolio sections with no content are hidden by default -- tap 'Add more sections' to reveal them when you're ready.

items.title: Skills label updated
items.description: 'Skills Visibility' is now 'Skills on your portfolio' with a clearer count of highlighted skills.

items.title: Install banner stays out of the way
items.description: The PWA install prompt now sits just above the tab bar on small phones instead of floating over your content.

items.title: Better empty state for Applications
items.description: When you have no tracked applications, a clear centered prompt helps you add your first one.

items.title: Job titles are always readable
items.description: Truncated job titles on application cards now reveal the full name on long-press or hover.

summary: A precision release — every button, form, and tap audited and fixed. The small things that felt slightly off are now exactly right.

items.title: One tap to edit
items.description: Tapping 'Edit' now takes you straight into the editor — no intermediate screen.

items.title: Tailor & Interview from Resume Detail
items.description: Both actions now appear on the Resume Detail page and correctly load your resume into context before navigating.

items.title: ATS Improve opens your resume
items.description: The Improve shortcut in your ATS breakdown now always loads the correct resume.

items.title: Shortcuts go exactly where they say
items.description: All quick-action cards navigate correctly regardless of sign-in status.

items.title: Smart keyboards everywhere
items.description: Phone, URL, and email fields now trigger the right keyboard layout on mobile.

items.title: Signing out returns you home
items.description: Sign-out now lands on the home screen — not the login page.

summary: Your resume is now a full personal website — with themes, AI-written content, and a shareable link you can send to anyone.

items.title: Public Portfolio Website
items.description: Turn your resume into a beautiful personal site in one tap and share a real link with recruiters or clients.

items.title: Portfolio themes & hero redesign
items.description: Bold hero, animated avatar glow, sticky scroll header, and four curated themes: Minimal, Bold Dark, Glass Pro, Classic Clean.

items.title: Case Studies & Services sections
items.description: Portfolio-only content blocks with challenge, outcome, tech used, and optional pricing.

items.title: Resume ↔ Portfolio sync mode
items.description: Choose Auto-sync (live) or Locked (snapshot) so your portfolio stays stable while you keep editing.

items.title: AI portfolio content helpers
items.description: AI can write your About section, a Case Study from a project, and availability headlines — with preview before applying.

summary: Reliability and resilience — your work is safer, your data smarter about conflicts, and the experience tighter on mobile.

items.title: Unsaved changes guard
items.description: The editor detects unsaved changes and prompts Save, Discard, or Cancel before you navigate away — including Android back button.

items.title: Offline sync conflict detection
items.description: If a resume changed on another device, a clear dialog lets you pick the server or local version.

items.title: 44px touch targets everywhere
items.description: All interactive elements in the editor and tracker now meet the mobile accessibility standard.

items.title: Mobile delete confirmation
items.description: Deleting an application now opens a thumb-friendly bottom drawer instead of triggering immediately.

summary: AI status at a glance, 30+ templates, new editor tools, and a settings page rebuilt from scratch.

items.title: Real-time AI health indicator
items.description: A live status badge on AI-powered pages lets you know if the AI is ready or experiencing slowdowns.

items.title: 30+ resume templates
items.description: Template library expanded to 30+ professional designs across every industry and career level.

items.title: Settings page overhaul
items.description: Added sign-out confirmation, change password, privacy toggles, account stats card, and guest user prompt.

items.title: Version history, content library & proofread
items.description: Three new editor tools: restore past snapshots, reuse saved content blocks, and run a full AI proofread.

summary: The tailoring experience got a complete animation and feedback overhaul — and mobile scrolling works the way it should.

items.title: Polished tailor loading screen
items.description: Real-feel progress animation with cubic ease, animated percentage, glowing bar, ETA countdown, and fun facts carousel.

items.title: Projected score preview
items.description: Live before/after ATS score comparison and skill gap count shown during the tailoring process.

items.title: Mobile scroll fixes
items.description: Fixed non-scrollable pages on Job Details, Application Details, Cover Letters, and Notifications.

summary: Your career toolkit — plan your path, close your skill gaps, and write your resignation letter with AI.

items.title: Career Path Advisor
items.description: AI-powered career roadmap with milestones, timeline, and recommended next roles based on your resume.

items.title: Skill Gap Analyzer
items.description: See exactly which skills you're missing for your target role and get learning recommendations.

items.title: Resignation Letter generator
items.description: AI drafts your resignation letter with tone selection, notice period, and a pre-departure checklist.

items.title: Career Guides library
items.description: Curated guides on resumes, interviews, and job searching — readable in-app, no browser needed.

items.title: Command Palette
items.description: Press ⌘K anywhere to instantly jump to any page or action in the app.

summary: AI Studio launched — five specialized AI tools for the serious job seeker, all in one hub.

items.title: AI Studio hub
items.description: Central AI workspace with Wise AI Chat, quick tools, and one-tap access to every AI feature.

items.title: Recruiter Simulation
items.description: A simulated recruiter reviews your resume and tells you what would make them move forward — or not.

items.title: LinkedIn Optimizer
items.description: Adapt your resume content for LinkedIn with tailored headline, summary, and bullet rewrites.

items.title: A/B Resume Compare
items.description: Compare two resume versions side-by-side with score breakdown and section-level diffs.

items.title: AI Content Detector
items.description: Detect AI-sounding language in your resume and humanize it with one tap.

items.title: One-Page Wizard
items.description: AI compresses your resume to one page while preserving all the content that matters.

summary: Track every application, generate cover letters instantly, and stay on top of your job search — all in one place.

items.title: Job Application Tracker
items.description: Track every application through Saved → Applied → Interview → Offer with an activity timeline and follow-up reminders.

items.title: Follow-up email composer
items.description: AI drafts a follow-up email for any application with the right tone for the stage you're at.

items.title: Cover Letters
items.description: AI generates a tailored cover letter from your resume and a job description — editable, exportable as PDF.

items.title: Job Match Score
items.description: Every saved job shows a match score against your primary resume before you even apply.

items.title: Notifications & Profile page
items.description: In-app notification center and a dedicated profile page with career level, open-to-work status, and social links.

summary: Get your existing resume into WiseResume in seconds — and browse real examples for inspiration.

items.title: PDF & DOCX import
items.description: Upload any resume PDF or Word document and WiseResume parses it into a fully editable resume.

items.title: OCR image import
items.description: Snap a photo of a printed resume and the OCR engine extracts all your content automatically.

items.title: Resume Examples Gallery
items.description: Browse curated resume examples by industry and experience level for inspiration or to use as a starting point.

items.title: Public share links
items.description: Share a read-only link to your resume with anyone — no account required to view it.

items.title: PWA install & offline mode
items.description: Install WiseResume as an app on your home screen and keep working even without a connection.

summary: WiseResume launches — a professional resume builder with AI writing, ATS scoring, and cloud sync.

items.title: AI-powered resume editor
items.description: Section-by-section editing with AI enhancement on every field — summary, bullets, skills, and more.

items.title: ATS scoring
items.description: Instant ATS compatibility score with keyword analysis and actionable improvement suggestions.

items.title: 12 professional templates
items.description: Clean, Modern, Classic, Executive, Developer, Creative, and six more — all ATS-safe.

items.title: PDF & DOCX export
items.description: Export your resume as a print-ready PDF or editable Word document in one tap.

items.title: AI Interview Prep
items.description: Practice with an AI interviewer that asks role-specific questions and scores your answers.

items.title: Cloud sync & biometric lock
items.description: Your data syncs across devices and can be secured with Face ID or fingerprint on supported devices.
